Theory under test: K q8_0 34B/block interleaved (2B scale + 32B qs) makes every qs read straddle a 32B transaction boundary; split-plane (qs plane [max_ctx][nblk][32B] perfectly aligned + separate 2B scale plane, mirror of the A6 weight repack) should cut sector traffic ~2x at DRAM depths. Probe: standalone probe/kv_align_probe.cu — verbatim copies of fa_decode_vec_q (register) and fa_decode_vec_q_smem (deep-ctx) with ONLY the K addressing remapped in the _al twins, 27B geometry (nh=24 nhkv=4 hd=256, grid/block/split-ladder mirror of the engine launcher), 8 rotating layer buffers so L2 behaves like the real multi-layer walk, partials verified BIT-IDENTICAL between layouts before timing (pure address remap, dequant math unchanged).

lesson: TRANSACTION-STRADDLE THEORY DIES ON THE MEASUREMENT: the aligned layout DOES cut L1 sector traffic exactly as theorized (1.22->1.07 sectors/request, -11% sectors) but the kernel duration is IDENTICAL (256.06 vs 256.03us at 32k) — fa_decode at deep ctx is LATENCY-bound (DRAM 15% of peak, warps 34%), not transaction-bound, so saved sectors buy zero time. Sector metrics alone cannot justify a layout change; the roofline position decides. Side observations: (a) reg-aligned wins +4.5% at 8k only (the one cell where reg is transaction-sensitive) — below the wire bar and 8k decode is not the blocker; (b) in this 8-layer-rotating synthetic the smem twin beats the register path 2.1-2.3x at EVERY depth incl 8k (68.7 vs 159.8us) — the engine's BW24_FA_SMEM_TKV=16384 crossover may be re-sweepable on the real trace (real decode has other kernels evicting L2 between FA calls, so the synthetic overstates it), possible cheap follow-up; (c) remaining ARC A room is NOT in KV addressing — the +18ms/tok decay vs 0.76ms wall-math must live in latency/occupancy structure (combine chain, n_splits tail, launch overhead) not byte traffic.

result.notes: PROBE VERDICT: GO — far past the 1.3x gate. Strongest card in the prefill arc so far. KEY DESIGN FACTS for the prototype: (1) the F8-E4M3 attn/linear-attn weights are ALREADY e4m3 in the checkpoint (per-tensor f32 weight_scale) — an FP8 GEMM path for them is EXACT on the weight side (no re-quant; today they get re-encoded Q8_0 and ride the 47-72TF int8 GEMM = the single biggest prefill sink at 46.5%); activation fp8-e4m3 per-token scale is the only new rounding vs today's q8_1 per-32 int8 (finer-grained scale, coarser mantissa — argmax gate must decide). (2) per-token act scale CANNOT ride the GEMM's B_SCALE (OUTER_VEC unsupported): fold per-token scale by scaling the f32 D rows post-GEMM (cheap, y is f32) or quantize per-tensor. (3) MLP NVFP4 layers: e2m1 codes are exactly representable in e4m3, but the per-16 ue4m3 scales cannot enter the GEMM (VEC16 unsupported) — folding scales into e4m3 codes loses weight bits (e4m3 has 3 mantissa bits, product scale*code needs more) => W4A16-style convert must be gated by the same maxdiff battery that closed W4A4; the SAFE first prototype is the F8-native q8_0-class layers only (~1.85x pp ceiling alone). (4) mxfp8 VEC32_UE8M0 IS available — a future rung could re-block NVFP4 (per-16 ue4m3) to mxfp8 (per-32 ue8m0 weight + per-32 act) entirely in cuBLASLt, worth its own probe. NEXT: prototype BW24_PP_FP8=1 keeping F8 weights as fp8-e4m3 device bytes (skip Q8_0 re-encode), per-token act quantize kernel (probe kernel exists), cuBLASLt TN f32-D + row-rescale, argmax MATCH gate, then pp512/2048/6257 N=3
